mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and credit-dependent sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side resilience, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of recovery regime observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI readings exceeding 218 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force tighter monetary policy expectations and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 218 are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates signals structural labor market deterioration and reduced aggregate income, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling payrolls and rising unemployment shows no predictive power for 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day returns in > 55% of expansionary cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices exceeding 108 signal peak manufacturing capacity utilization and strong near-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 108 are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 60% of late-cycle expansion observations.
